title: Implement tower extension field architecture for GF(p^n)
description:
Implement recursive tower extension fields for constructing GF(p^n) from smaller fields. This is the algebraic foundation for high-degree extension field arithmetic, following the tower construction pattern used in pairing-based cryptography. ## Architecture Tower extensions build complex fields recursively from simpler ones: ``` GF(p) → GF(p^2) → GF(p^6) → GF(p^12) degree 2 degree 3 degree 2 ``` Each level is an extension of the previous, keeping intermediate values small enough to fit in CPU registers and enabling hardcoded optimal multiplication formulas. ## Core types ```rust /// A quadratic extension: elements are a0 + a1·u where u^2 = non_residue. /// Uses Karatsuba multiplication (3 base multiplications instead of 4). pub struct QuadraticExt { c0: F, c1: F, } /// A cubic extension: elements are a0 + a1·v + a2·v^2. /// Uses Toom-Cook or Chung-Hasan multiplication (5 base multiplications instead of 9). pub struct CubicExt { c0: F, c1: F, c2: F, } ``` Both implement `FiniteField`. When the base field `F` is `ConstField + Copy`, the extension is also `ConstField + Copy`. ## Multiplication algorithms ### Karatsuba for degree-2 (QuadraticExt) For `(a0 + a1·u)(b0 + b1·u)`: 1. `v0 = a0 * b0` 2. `v1 = a1 * b1` 3. `v2 = (a0 + a1) * (b0 + b1)` 4. Result: `(v0 + β·v1) + (v2 - v0 - v1)·u` where β is the non-residue 3 base-field multiplications instead of 4 (25% reduction). ### Toom-Cook for degree-3 (CubicExt) 5 base-field multiplications instead of 9 (44% reduction). The Chung-Hasan SQR variant is even faster for squaring (fewer additions). ## Wide accumulator integration Tower extensions use **lazy/delayed reduction**: intermediate products at each tower level are accumulated in `Wide` types before reducing. This means: - `QuadraticExt>` accumulates in `(u128, u128)` — two wide components - Reduction happens at the Fp level, not at each tower level - The `max_unreduced_additions()` propagates through the tower: outer levels inherit the base field's reduction bound ## Concrete constructions to support | Field | Tower | Use case | |-------|-------|----------| | GF(p^2) | `QuadraticExt>` | Basic extension, complex-number analogy | | GF(p^4) | `QuadraticExt>>` | Double extension | | GF(p^6) | `CubicExt>>` | BLS12-381 intermediate | | GF(p^12) | `QuadraticExt>>>` | BLS12-381 target field | | GF(2^128) | `QuadraticExt` | AES-GCM / GHASH field | ## Location `crates/gf2-core/src/gfpn/` (new module) - `tower.rs` — `QuadraticExt`, `CubicExt` types and their `FiniteField` implementations - `non_residue.rs` — trait/const for specifying the irreducible polynomial at each tower level ## Dependencies - 8ce6f8aa (Montgomery multiplication) — the base field `Fp

` must be performant before building towers on top of it ## Success criteria - `QuadraticExt>` and `CubicExt>` pass the full field axiom test harness (2248b17d). - Nested towers (`QuadraticExt>>>`) pass axiom tests. - Karatsuba and Toom-Cook multiplication produce identical results to naive polynomial multiplication. - For `QuadraticExt>` with P fitting in 64 bits: multiplication completes in ≤10 base-field multiplications (3 Karatsuba + overhead).
